rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Summary Judgment, Memo Of P&A And Declaration Of Pltf
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 05, 2019, Line 1, PLAINTIFF PAUL O'GRADY's Motion To Set Aside Summary Judgment. Plaintiff Paul O'Grady's motion to set aside summary judgment is continued to January 8, 2020 so that it can be heard by Judge Schulman, who is not available to hear this motion on December 5, 2019. Because the motion seeks to set aside a ruling made by Judge Schulman, the motion should be heard by Judge Schulman. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PETER LEVY VS. CATALYST RESOURCE GROUP, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Enforce Written Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 05, 2019, Line 2, PLAINTIFF PETER LEVY's Motion To Enforce Written Settlement Agreement. Plaintiff Peter Levy's motion to enforce written settlement agreement is granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order and proposed judgment submitted by Mr. Levy.=(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LAURINE JONOPOLUS VS. AT&T ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Waiver Of Jury Trial
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 05, 2019, Line 4, PLAINTIFF LAURINE JONOPOLUS' Motion For Relief From Waiver Of Jury Trial. Plaintiff Laurine Jonopolus' motion for relief from waiver of jury is granted. No opposition filed and good cause shown. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: VICENTE ARTURO SOTO ET AL VS. LINO CAMPOS MELARA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; And Declartaion Of Lino Melara Campos In Support Of Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One And Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 5, 2019, Line 1, DEFENDANT LINO MELARA'S Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One And Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Adrienne Rogers, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to adrienneleight@hotmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: BRUCE MITCH FERRIN VS. CHRISTIAN C. WAIS II ET AL

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 5, 2019, Line 2, DEFENDANT CHRISTIAN WAIS II'S Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery Pro Tem Judge Adrienne Rogers, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required at 9 a.m. Moving party must bring a courtesy copy of the entire motion, which includes Exhibit C to Isaac Jacobsen's declaration (requests for admission, set one) and the proposed order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to adrienneleight@hotmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
